name: Xissira
body:
# Xissira



## Quick Info
| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Race/Class** | Dragonborn / Oathbreaker Paladin |
| **Faction** | The Spree Alliance — Right Hand of Valas |
| **Status** | Alive |
| **Location** | TBD |
| **First Appeared** | TBD |
| **Relationship** | Hostile — complicated |
| **CR/Level** | TBD — [D&D Beyond](https://www.dndbeyond.com/monsters/2121278-xissira) |

---

## Appearance
A dragonborn with the bearing of someone who has spent a lifetime earning the fear she receives. Heavy armor, dragon-scale features, the symbol of Tiamat somewhere on her body — tattooed or branded. She stands with a presence that makes the space around her feel like her territory. Dragons and dragonborn who know her history give her a wide berth. She has earned that.

---

## Personality
Ruthless, cunning, strategic. Xissira has never stopped asking herself whether what she's doing is right — she just keeps answering "yes" even as the acts get darker. She is genuinely complex: a person who experienced real injustice and real betrayal, who made real choices to become something she wasn't raised to be, and who believes — or needs to believe — that she is on the path of righteousness. She is wrong. She knows this, somewhere. She pushes it down.

She is Valas's right hand, his enforcer, his champion. She travels the realms punishing those who fail the Spree's cause. Her savagery is purposeful, not random.

---

## Motivation
To further Valas's goal of destroying the gods and to honor Tiamat, who gave her a place of respect when no one else would. She is also driven by a hunger for power, recognition, and the vindication of every dragonborn who was ever treated as lesser. She believes — genuinely — that what she's doing is justice for her people.

---

## Secret
**DM ONLY**
> - **Xissira trained [[Amaris Vendanii]]**. When Amaris was beaten nearly to death in a gutter after her father's death, it was Xissira who found her, recognized her potential, and took her under her wing. She trained Amaris as a paladin. Amaris's strength, her fighting style, her faith — all of it has Xissira's fingerprints on it.
> - Xissira now serves the organization dedicated to destroying Helm and everything Amaris's faith stands for. This is not a coincidence to be ignored — it is the campaign's most personal villain reveal for Amaris's player.
> - Xissira came from House Invincible — the same Church of Helm that Amaris grew up in. She served there. She broke her oath there. She watched the same institution that sheltered Amaris and its corruption firsthand.
> - Her path from Helmite warrior to Tiamat's consort to Valas's right hand is internally consistent: she was betrayed by Helm's institution, given power and dignity by Tiamat, and offered purpose by Valas. Every step made sense at the time. That's what makes her tragic.
> - She may still feel something toward Amaris — not regret exactly, but a recognition of what she made. She shaped the paladin who may ultimately be sent to stop her.

---

## Relationships
- [[Valas Halfinas]] — Right-hand; loyal operative; true believer in his cause
- [[Amaris Vendanii]] — Former student; trained her as a paladin; does not know she is now opposing the Spree
- Tiamat — Patron deity; consort; the one who first gave her respect
- [[The Spree Alliance]] — Senior member; enforcer; champion
- House Invincible (Church of Helm) — Former affiliation; broke her oath here

---

## History
Born a dragonborn into a clan serving House Invincible, where she trained as a warrior and rose to Master at Arms. She saw the hypocrisy within House Invincible — the discrimination against her and her kind — and began to question her faith in Helm. In battle, she slew Kasarus, a paladin of Tiamat, only to learn he was Tiamat's consort. Tiamat, rather than punishing her, began to corrupt her mind and test her faith — offering her a place of respect and power. She broke her oath to Helm and became Tiamat's consort and champion.

When she encountered Valas Halfinas, she saw a mission that aligned with everything she believed: destroy the gods who had allowed her people to be treated as lesser. She became his right hand.

Somewhere in the middle of all of this, she found a dying Aasimar girl bleeding out in a gutter in Silverymoon. She recognized potential. She trained her. That girl became Amaris Vendanii.

---

## Sessions Appeared In
- TBD

---

## Connected Factions
- [[The Spree Alliance]]
- Church of Tiamat
- House Invincible (Church of Helm) — former

---

## DM Notes
**Private**
> - Xissira is Amaris's personal villain. This confrontation needs to land. Plant the seeds early — maybe the party finds evidence of Xissira's involvement without knowing yet that it's someone Amaris knows.
> - Her complexity is the point. She is not evil because she enjoys it. She is evil because she convinced herself it was right, and the conviction is real. That makes her much harder to simply defeat.
> - Consider a moment where she recognizes Amaris (or Amaris's fighting style) before Amaris recognizes her. What does she do with that recognition?
> - Her endgame confrontation with Amaris should be emotional, not just tactical — a conversation mid-fight, something that forces Amaris to reckon with who trained her and what that means.

name: Aurgolarossa
body:
# Aurgolarossa



## Quick Info
| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Race/Class** | Shadow Dragon (Dracolich / Demigod) |
| **Faction** | Independent — true master behind the Spree Alliance |
| **Status** | Alive — Transformed (Dracolich, Demigod) |
| **Location** | Unknown |
| **First Appeared** | TBD |
| **Relationship** | Hostile (agenda concealed) |
| **CR/Level** | TBD — effectively demigod tier |

---

## Appearance
An ancient shadow dracolich of immense size. In her true form she is a dragon wreathed in shadow energy — scales once black or deep grey now spectral and translucent, trailing wisps of shadow. Her eyes glow with cold silver light. She is rarely seen directly; she prefers agents, whispers, and proxies.

Also known by her former title: **the Sibilant Shade**.

---

## Personality
Exceptionally patient, highly intelligent, and utterly ruthless. Aurgolarossa thinks in centuries and plans accordingly. She is a masterful manipulator — she whispers to priests, corrupts institutions, and engineers wars rather than fighting them herself. She views mortals and even other powerful beings as instruments. She is obsessed with death, undeath, and shadow magic, and she considers herself the foremost authority on all three.

---

## Motivation
Her ultimate goal is to **control death itself** — specifically, to dismantle the divine infrastructure that governs the cycle of death and afterlife. She seeks to achieve demigod (and beyond) status by becoming the draconic death deity she always believed herself destined to be. To this end, she has been orchestrating a campaign to weaken and destroy the gods from behind the scenes, using the Spree Alliance and Valas Halfinas as unwitting instruments.

---

## Secret
**DM ONLY**
> - Aurgolarossa is the **true villain** of the campaign. Everything the party has encountered — the Spree Alliance, Valas's rise, the attacks on the gods — traces back to her.
> - She has been **manipulating Valas Halfinas** (Merrick) since before his emergence from Barovia. Valas believes he is the architect of the Spree; he is her most effective pawn.
> - Her true goal is not political domination — it is **dismantling divine infrastructure**: the planes of the dead, the Weave, the machinery of divine power. She wants to become the sole arbiter of death.
> - Also known historically as **Tazenlimar** — a name she has assumed in her new existence as a demigod.
> - The campaign's confirmed tragic resolution: **Aurgolarossa's agenda is revealed too late** — by the time the party understands the full scope of her plan, the damage is already done.
> - She has achieved demigod status. She is not simply a powerful monster — she is a nascent deity of death and shadow.

---

## Relationships
- [[Valas Halfinas]] — Her primary pawn. He does not know she exists as the true power behind his throne.
- [[The Spree Alliance]] — Organization she created and controls through proxies.
- [[Null]] — The draconic death god she has worshipped and sought to emulate/surpass.
- [[Dumathoin]] — Dwarven god of secrets and the dead; she subverted his priesthood at Thunderholme.

---

## History
Born in 426 DR during the Year of the Black Dawn, Aurgolarossa spent her early centuries establishing a lair in the Thunder Peaks and cultivating a reputation for cunning rather than brute force. She was a worshipper of Null, the draconic death god, and harbored ambitions to become his consort and achieve demipower status.

In 989 DR she became aware of the dwarven city of Thunderholme and began a decades-long campaign to corrupt it from within — whispering to the high priest of Dumathoin, slowly steering him toward madness and devotion to Null. In 1054 DR she emerged at the grand temple dedication and slaughtered the city's population, raising the dead as undead servants. Thunderholme fell.

She underwent the transformation to dracolich in 1322 DR, manipulating the Cult of the Dragon into performing the ritual while engineering enough control to remain independent of their binding.

Over the following centuries she accumulated power, influence, and arcane knowledge — orchestrating merchant wars in Sembia, stealing archmage libraries, and quietly placing agents throughout Faerûn. By the time the campaign begins, she has achieved demigod status and is operating as the hidden hand behind the Spree Alliance's war on the gods.

---

## Sessions Appeared In
- TBD

---

## Connected Locations
- [[Thunder Peaks]] — site of her original lair
- [[Thunderholme]] — dwarven city she destroyed
- [[Sembia]] — historical sphere of influence
- [[Shadowfell]] — domain of her shadow magic

---

## Connected Factions
- [[The Spree Alliance]] — secretly controls
- [[Cult of the Dragon]] — former affiliation, eventually took over the Sembian cell

---

## Connected Artifacts
- TBD — likely possesses artifacts from Thunderholme and the stolen library of Mhzentul

---

## DM Notes
**Private**
> - Aurgolarossa should never feel present until the players are deep into the endgame. She is the shadow behind the shadow.
> - Build her reveal gradually: first the party notices patterns that don't fit Valas's apparent agenda. Then they find evidence of a third party. Then her name surfaces in lore.
> - Her demigod status means a final confrontation cannot be won by combat alone — consider what "defeating" her actually means in the endgame.
> - The tragedy of the campaign is partly that she wins: her agenda succeeds to a degree, even if she is ultimately stopped. What does the world look like after her plan partially unfolds?

name: Valas Halfinas
body:
# Valas Halfinas



## Quick Info
| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Race/Class** | Fallen Aasimar / Fallen Cleric (Vampire Lord) |
| **Faction** | The Spree Alliance |
| **Status** | Alive — Transformed (Vampire Lord) |
| **Location** | Unknown — operates from the shadows |
| **First Appeared** | TBD |
| **Relationship** | Hostile |
| **CR/Level** | TBD — [D&D Beyond](https://www.dndbeyond.com/monsters/2606775-valas-halfinas) |

---

## Appearance
Tall, with light olive skin, light blue eyes, and short black hair. Adorned in gleaming blackened steel full plate with a cloak of midnight black that seems to move as if alive, absorbing the light around it. Wields a longsword that pulsates with dark energy and carries a tower shield rimmed with strips of human flesh.

---

## Personality
Cold, calculating, and consumed by resentment. Valas is driven by a deep hatred of the gods — especially Helm — whom he blames for his abandonment and corruption. He operates through manipulation and deceit, preferring to let lieutenants act in his name while he pulls strings from the shadows. Despite his monstrous transformation, traces of his former devotion and discipline remain, twisted into something terrifying.

---

## Motivation
Wants to destroy the gods, who he blames for his abandonment and corruption. Seeks to create a world free from divine influence — a world where mortals are no longer subject to the will of gods who can abandon or betray them. Uses manipulation, terror, and the corruption of souls as instruments of his agenda.

---

## Secret
**DM ONLY**
> - Valas Halfinas's true identity is **Merrick** — the name he took after 500 years trapped in Barovia where he transformed into a dreadlord and then vampire lord.
> - He was a former cleric of **Helm** in Helm's Hold, raised off the streets by **Severinus Acustes** — who is, unknown to Valas, the mortal avatar of Helm himself.
> - Valas has been **unknowingly manipulated by Aurgolarossa** throughout his rise to power. He believes he is leading the Spree Alliance; he is, in fact, a pawn.
> - The campaign's confirmed tragic resolution: **Valas dies unredeemed.** He never learns the full truth about Severinus, never reconciles his past, and never escapes Aurgolarossa's shadow.
> - The Mantle Theory is canonical — Valas's corruption was shaped by his relationship with Helm's divine role as Watcher, which he inverted into obsessive vigilance against the gods themselves.

---

## Relationships
- [[Severinus Acustes]] — Former mentor and father figure. Unknowingly the mortal incarnation of Helm. Valas does not know this.
- [[Aurgolarossa]] — Secret puppetmaster. Valas believes he acts independently; Aurgolarossa has been pulling strings for decades.
- [[Amaris Vendanii]] — PC paladin carrying Helm's power via the Tome of the Undying Watch. Thematically linked to Valas's abandoned faith.
- [[Mourn Eveningfall]] — TBD relationship

---

## History
Valas was an Aasimar orphan from Eigersstor who endured a childhood of neglect and abuse until the age of six, when he was caught stealing and rescued by Severinus Acustes — a holy man who brought him into the Church of Helm. Under Severinus's guidance, Valas rose steadily through the church's ranks, becoming known for his combat prowess and healing abilities, despite ongoing struggles with aggression and addiction.

He eventually attained the rank of Bishop. His peaceful life ended when he was summoned by the High Mage of Silverymoon for an adventure — and vanished. Helm's Hold mourned his loss. A ballad was written: *"The Holy Flame Extinguished."*

What the world did not know: Valas became trapped in Barovia for 500 years, enduring a torturous existence that transformed him into the dreadlord known as **Merrick**. Consumed by resentment toward the gods — and toward Helm and Severinus specifically — he emerged from Barovia with a singular purpose: to dismantle divine power and avenge his abandonment. He now leads the Spree Alliance under the alias Merrick, operating from the shadows and orchestrating events across Faerûn.

---

## Sessions Appeared In
- TBD

---

## Connected Locations
- [[Eigersstor]] — birthplace
- [[Helm's Hold]] — where he was raised and served
- [[Barovia]] — site of his 500-year imprisonment and transformation
- [[Silverymoon]] — last known destination before disappearance

---

## Connected Factions
- [[The Spree Alliance]] — leads under alias Merrick
- [[Church of Helm]] — former affiliation

---

## Connected Artifacts
- Longsword (dark energy, unnamed) — TBD
- Tower shield rimmed with human flesh — TBD

---

## DM Notes
**Private**
> - Valas is the emotional core of the campaign's tragic arc. He should never be pure villain — his grief and abandonment must remain legible underneath the monster.
> - Consider scenes or flashbacks that let the party glimpse who he was before Barovia.
> - His death should feel like a loss, not a victory — the tragedy is that he was never given the chance to be saved.
> - Aurgolarossa's manipulation of Valas is the campaign's deepest secret — reveal it only at the endgame.
